channel.title: AI Unfiltered
channel.description: Chinese AI • Open Source • Security • Incidents. Signal, not noise.

item.title: Network Performance issues - Increased HTTP 502 Errors in HKG
item.description: Jul 15, 11:00 UTC Monitoring - Cloudflare is investigating an increased level of HTTP 502 errors originating from the Hong Kong datacenter. We are working to analyse and mitigate this problem. More updates to follow shortly.

item.title: Windows Bind Link Attacks Can Hide Malware From EDR Tools
item.description: Bitdefender researchers show how Windows bind links can create conflicting filesystem views to hide malware from endpoint security products. The post Windows Bind Link Attacks Can Hide Malware From EDR Tools appeared first on SecurityWeek.

item.title: The Download: a useful quantum machine and a record-breaking subsea tunnel
item.description: This is today’s edition of The Download, our weekday newsletter that provides a daily dose of what’s going on in the world of technology. PsiQuantum has a plan to make a massive quantum computer out of light The machine that could change the world will be housed in a room that looks...

item.title: Helping small businesses with free, hands-on cyber consultancy
item.description: Cyber Advisors are offering free 30-minute consultations to help small businesses get started with cyber security.

item.title: SASE Has An AI Blind Spot. Inspecting Packets Is No Longer Enough.
item.description: For years, routing traffic through cloud proxies was good enough. Then work moved to the browser, AI entered the workflow, and the inspection model stopped keeping up. Enterprise workflows now live across SaaS applications, browsers, and an expanding ecosystem of generative AI tools, unsanctioned...

item.title: Researcher Drops New Windows Zero-Day PoC Hours After Microsoft Patch Tuesday
item.description: Security researcher Chaotic Eclipse (aka Nightmare-Eclipse) has released a new proof-of-concept (PoC) exploit called LegacyHive. It has been described as a Windows User Profile Service arbitrary hive load elevation of privileges vulnerability. The Windows User Profile Service, also referred to as...

item.title: New Webinar: Closing the Approval Gap in AI-Era Ad Tech
item.description: A single approved marketing tag can quietly load fourth-party code your security team has never seen, granting full access to your forms, customer data, and checkout pages. This on-demand webinar reveals how this Approval Gap forms, and gives your team the blueprint to close it before an auditor,...

item.title: Progress Confirms Zero-Day Vulnerability Behind ShareFile Disruption
item.description: The company has rolled out a fix and is restoring access for Storage Zones Controller customers who apply it. The post Progress Confirms Zero-Day Vulnerability Behind ShareFile Disruption appeared first on SecurityWeek.

item.title: ICS Patch Tuesday: Vulnerabilities Fixed by Siemens, Schneider, Rockwell
item.description: The industrial giants fixed dozens of vulnerabilities across their ICS products, with advisories also released by CISA and VDE CERT. The post ICS Patch Tuesday: Vulnerabilities Fixed by Siemens, Schneider, Rockwell appeared first on SecurityWeek.

item.title: Compromised AsyncAPI npm Packages Deliver Multi-Stage Botnet Malware
item.description: Four compromised npm packages in the @asyncapi namespace have been observed distributing a multi-stage botnet loader, according to findings from OX Security, SafeDep, Socket, and StepSecurity. The affected packages are listed below - @asyncapi/generator-helpers@1.1.1...

item.title: High-Purity PFA Becomes Critical Semiconductor Material as Chinese Suppliers Advance Batch Verification for Process Node Localization
item.description: High-purity PFA, a fluoropolymer essential for advanced semiconductor wet processing equipment, sees batch verification by Chinese suppliers as the semiconductor supply chain localization push deepens.

item.title: Hangzhou Xunsun Intelligence CEO: AI Software Factory Redefines Software Production From Requirements to Running Systems
item.description: Xunsun Intelligence CEO Xiong Jibin at AgenticAICon 2026 unveils AI-Ready requirements modeler and software factory, enabling natural language to full system generation with multi-agent collaboration.

item.title: From Computing Power to Intelligence: How Data Is Reshaping the AI Era in 2026
item.description: As compute race matures, industry focus shifts to data as the fuel of AI intelligence — covering data asset monetization, security governance, and AI-driven scientific research paradigm changes.

item.title: Xiaomi Open-Sources Robotics-U0: A 38B-Parameter Embodied Generative Model That Unifies Four Robot Tasks
item.description: Xiaomi releases Robotics-U0 with 38 billion parameters, the first embodied generative model handling scene generation, embodied transfer, video generation, and text-to-image in a single unified architecture.

item.title: Critical Vulnerabilities Patched With Fresh Chrome 150, Firefox 152 Updates
item.description: Public exploit code targeting the Firefox flaws exists, but no in-the-wild exploitation has been observed. The post Critical Vulnerabilities Patched With Fresh Chrome 150, Firefox 152 Updates appeared first on SecurityWeek.

item.title: Two SonicWall SMA 1000 Zero-Days Exploited, One Could Enable Admin Commands
item.description: SonicWall has warned of active exploitation of two zero-day vulnerabilities impacting Secure Mobile Access (SMA) 1000 series appliances, one of which could be exploited to achieve arbitrary command execution. The vulnerabilities are listed below - CVE-2026-15409 (CVSS score: 10.0) - A Server-side...

item.title: SonicWall Issues Urgent SMA Patch Warning for Two Zero-Day Exploits
item.description: SonicWall SMA1000 zero-day vulnerabilities CVE-2026-15409 and CVE-2026-15410 can be exploited for remote code execution. The post SonicWall Issues Urgent SMA Patch Warning for Two Zero-Day Exploits appeared first on SecurityWeek.

item.title: OmniPMNet: Bridging discrete and gridded PM10 forecasts via omni-query neural processes
item.description: arXiv:2607.11896v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Forecasting particulate matter (PM10) requires both station-scale accuracy and continuous spatial fields, especially during severe dust storms. Chemical transport models (CTMs) provide gridded forecasts but retain local biases, whereas graph neural...

item.title: Semidirect Fourier Delta Attention: Phase-Controlled Delta Memory with Constructive Chunk-WY Kernels
item.description: arXiv:2607.11897v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Linear attention replaces softmax attention's growing KV cache with a fixed recurrent state, but this compression limits exact state tracking and long-context memory. We introduce \emph{Semidirect Fourier Delta Attention} (SFDA), a phase-controlled...

item.title: Repairing Shape-Prior Shortcuts in Long-Range Single-Shot Fringe Projection Profilometry
item.description: arXiv:2607.11928v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Single-shot fringe projection profilometry (FPP) networks that regress depth directly can exploit a shape-prior shortcut, recovering depth from object boundaries rather than from fringe phase. On a photorealistic synthetic benchmark (15,600 fringe...

item.title: Qubit-Efficient Quantum Search for Hyperdimensional Decomposition via Logarithmic Encoding
item.description: arXiv:2607.11936v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Hyperdimensional Computing (HDC) represents symbols using high-dimensional hypervectors of dimension $D$. In hypervector decomposition, the objective is to recover $F$ constituent hypervectors, each drawn from a codebook of size $N$, from a bound...

item.title: Mirror Horizon: Viable Path Entropy as a Measure of Bounded Reflection
item.description: arXiv:2607.11937v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Mirror Theory proposes that an intelligent system should be studied not only by what it represents, but by what coherent continuations it can sustain under repeated reflection. We make this claim operational through \emph{viable path entropy} (VPE), a...

item.title: Scaling Point-in-Time Language Models
item.description: arXiv:2607.11889v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Large language models trained on unrestricted internet corpora inevitably embed information from the future, introducing lookahead bias that compromises the validity of backtests and causal inference in finance and the social sciences. Point-in-time...

item.title: CANDI: Contextual Alignment for Niche Domains Question Answering
item.description: arXiv:2607.11891v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: The deployment of large language models (LLMs) in specialized domains like medical diagnostics and financial advisory necessitates evaluating capabilities beyond general knowledge. Traditional question-answering benchmarks often fail to capture the...

item.title: G-SHARE: A Guideline-Based Structured Reasoning Framework for Human-Factor Event Diagnosis
item.description: arXiv:2607.11892v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Human-factor event diagnosis is essential for learning from operational events in nuclear power plants, yet its quality depends strongly on expert interpretation of narrative reports and guideline-based reasoning.Existing data-driven or one-shot large...

item.title: I'm Sorry, but I Can't Help with Braille: Revealing Accessibility Failures in State-of-the-Art LLMs
item.description: arXiv:2607.11893v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Large Language Models (LLMs) perform strongly on many language tasks, but their capability in structurally constrained, accessibility-critical modalities such as Braille remains unclear. We evaluate state-of-the-art LLMs on bidirectional...

item.title: Graph-Based Detection of Disinformation Narrative Diffusion between Russian and Ukrainian Telegram Channels
item.description: arXiv:2607.11894v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Detecting disinformation narratives on social media is challenging due to the scale of amplification, rapid evolution, and linguistic variability of online content. We propose a graph-based framework for identifying and analyzing disinformation...

item.title: Shenzhen Professor Robot Startup LimX Dynamics Hits $2.3B Valuation With $200M Pre-IPO Round
item.description: Shenzhen-based LimX Dynamics, founded by Southern University of Science and Technology professor Zhang Wei, raises $200M at $2.3B valuation, pursuing non-factory humanoid robot strategy.

item.title: Elevated errors affecting ChatGPT
item.description: Status: ResolvedAll impacted services have now fully recovered.Affected components Image Generation (Operational) GPTs (Operational) Codex in ChatGPT Desktop (Operational) ChatGPT Work (Operational) Login (Operational) File uploads (Operational) Compliance API (Operational) Deep Research...

item.title: Issue affecting voice mode in ChatGPT
item.description: Status: ResolvedAll impacted services have now fully recovered.Affected components Voice mode (Operational)

item.title: Introducing Real World VoiceEQ: Measuring the human quality of voice AI
[AINews] not much happened today
item.description: a continuation: Codex adding 1M users a day now.

item.title: 5 Trends That Defined AI Engineering at World’s Fair 2026
item.description: At this year's AIE World’s Fair, AI engineering entered a new phase: building systems around agents, rather than just building with agents.

item.title: Partial outage of claude.ai: container creation
item.description: Jul 14, 22:16 UTC Resolved - This incident has been resolved. Jul 14, 21:58 UTC Monitoring - A fix has been implemented and we are monitoring the results Jul 14, 21:56 UTC Update - We are continuing to investigate this issue. Jul 14, 21:31 UTC Investigating - We are investigating an outage...

item.title: UPDATE: Google Cloud VMware Engine (GCVE) Stretched Cluster customers are experiencing zonal outages impacting network connectivity across multiple regions.
item.description: Incident began at 2026-07-14 10:00 (all times are US/Pacific).Summary Google Cloud VMware Engine (GCVE) Stretched Cluster customers are experiencing zonal outages impacting network connectivity across multiple regions. Description We are experiencing an issue with Google Cloud VMware Engine (GCVE)...

item.title: Microsoft Patches a Record 570 Security Flaws
item.description: Microsoft Corp. today released software updates to plug at least 570 security holes in its Windows operating systems and other software, almost triple the number of vulnerabilities the software giant fixed in its record-smashing Patch Tuesday release last month. Microsoft attributed the burgeoning...

item.title: Incident with Webhooks
item.description: Jul 14, 18:01 UTC Resolved - This incident has been resolved. Thank you for your patience and understanding as we addressed this issue. A detailed root cause analysis will be shared as soon as it is available. Jul 14, 17:55 UTC Monitoring - The degradation affecting Webhooks has been mitigated. We...

item.title: Some ChatGPT Go users may experience conversation failures when using GPT-5.5.
item.description: Status: ResolvedAll impacted services have now fully recovered.Affected components Conversations (Operational)

item.title: Elevated number of 502 errors for CDN/Cache in SIN
item.description: Jul 14, 16:00 UTC Resolved - Between 16:52-17:10 UTC today, customers would have experienced an elevated number of 502 errors from our CDN/Cache and Durable Objects in SIN.

item.title: Elevated R2 errors in WEUR region
item.description: Jul 14, 15:38 UTC Identified - The issue has been identified and a fix is being implemented. Jul 14, 15:23 UTC Investigating - Customers may experience elevated error rates in WEUR region

item.title: Energy-Based Physics-Informed Form Finding for Clustered Tensegrity Structures
item.description: Tensegrity form-finding and physical property prediction are fundamental inverse problems in structural mechanics, which aim to determine equilibrium configurations and internal force distributions. These problems are challenging due to strong nonlinearity arising from the coupling between geometry...

item.title: Can LLMs Write Reliable Rubrics? A Meta-Evaluation for Experiment Reproduction
item.description: Rubric-based evaluation is a promising approach for assessing open-ended outputs from LLM-based research agents, particularly in paper reproduction, where direct paper-to-repository comparison is prone to hallucination. However, constructing paper-specific rubrics requires substantial expert...

item.title: Gateway List updates using CSV not applied
item.description: Jul 14, 12:52 UTC Resolved - This incident has been resolved. Jul 14, 12:41 UTC Monitoring - A fix has been implemented and we are monitoring the results. Jul 14, 11:55 UTC Identified - Cloudflare is aware of and investigating an issue where changes applied via CSV to Gateway Lists are not being...

item.title: The Download: Claude’s inner workings, and the future of world models
item.description: This is today’s edition of The Download, our weekday newsletter that provides a daily dose of what’s going on in the world of technology. What Anthropic’s latest AI discovery does—and doesn’t—show —James O’Donnell When Anthropic announced last week that it had found a new window...

item.title: Atomic Units of X: The Compression Layer of Intelligence
item.description: This paper proposes a theoretical framework for understanding intelligence as a process of atomic compression and compositional reuse. We argue that cognitive, biological, computational, and organizational systems achieve scalable intelligence by decomposing complex phenomena into reusable atomic...

item.title: Disruption with some GitHub services
item.description: Jul 14, 09:56 UTC Resolved - This incident has been resolved. Thank you for your patience and understanding as we addressed this issue. A detailed root cause analysis will be shared as soon as it is available. Jul 14, 09:51 UTC Monitoring - The degradation affecting Codespaces has been mitigated....

item.title: PsiQuantum has a plan to make a massive quantum computer out of light
item.description: The machine that could change the world will be housed in a room that looks like a data center crossed with an ice cream factory. Inside will be some 100 stainless-steel cabinets, each about six feet tall and connected to a supply of liquid helium that keeps them only a few degrees above absolute...

item.title: EVOQUANT: Self-Evolving Verifier-Guided Strategy Optimization for Robust Quantitative Trading
item.description: Quantitative strategy optimization remains largely manual, requiring domain experts to identify weak signals, tune risk-control rules, and repeatedly validate iterative revisions. Large language models can accelerate this process, but directly relying on them to rewrite trading strategies often...

item.title: The Computational Basis of Confidence in Large Language Models
item.description: Reliable confidence -- the probability that a model's own answer is correct -- is essential for the trustworthy deployment of language models. Existing work has largely evaluated confidence by how well it predicts correctness and whether it is calibrated, leaving open a more fundamental question:...

item.title: Network Performance Issues in Haikou, China (HAK)
item.description: Jul 14, 06:30 UTC Resolved - Earlier today Cloudflare experienced network degradation in Haikou, China that affected some users. The period of impact was roughly between 14th July 2026, 05:45 - 06:07 UTC. Those issues have been resolved.
